message:
perf(raptorq): precompute GF(256) nibble multiplication tables as compile-time statics

Replace the per-call mul_nibble_tables() function (which built two
[u8; 16] arrays on every SIMD multiply invocation) with a single
build_mul_nibble_tables() const fn that precomputes the full 256x16
lookup tables at compile time. The result is stored in a static
MUL_NIBBLE_TABLES: ([[u8; 16]; 256], [[u8; 16]; 256]).

The runtime mul_nibble_tables(c) now returns (&'static [u8; 16],
&'static [u8; 16]) — a pair of references into the precomputed
static — instead of owned arrays. This changes the signature at
all call sites (gf256_mul_slice_x86_avx2_impl, the aarch64 NEON
variants, gf256_mul_slices2, gf256_addmul_slices2) from passing
&low_tbl_arr/&high_tbl_arr to passing the static references directly,
eliminating one level of indirection.

This optimization matters because GF(256) multiply is the innermost
loop of RaptorQ encoding/decoding — the nibble table construction
was repeated millions of times during bulk symbol processing. Moving
it to a compile-time static eliminates that per-symbol overhead entirely.

Also fixes minor formatting in raptorq/pipeline.rs (sender_pool_bounds
argument alignment).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
